title: Capital intensive and labour intensive operations
summary: 9609 AS — choosing capital vs labour intensity, cost structures, and contextual factors.
learningObjectives: Define capital intensive and labour intensive operations.
Explain factors affecting the choice between them.
Apply to manufacturing and service case studies.

simpleExplanation.title: Machines vs people
simpleExplanation.summary: Capital-intensive firms invest heavily in machinery (high fixed costs, low marginal cost). Labour-intensive firms rely on workers (lower setup cost, higher variable labour cost).
steps: Capital intensive: automation, robotics, high fixed costs.
Labour intensive: more workers relative to machinery.
Wage rates and technology prices drive the choice.

flashcards.back: High proportion of capital (machinery) to labour in production.
High proportion of labour costs — e.g. hand tailoring, care homes.
Capital intensive = higher fixed costs → higher break-even (5.4.4).
High volume, stable demand, expensive labour, precision needed.
Low volume, custom work, low wages, flexible skills.
High initial investment; obsolescence; redundancy costs.
Lower wages favour labour intensity vs automated rich markets.

sections.content: **Capital vs labour intensity** (4.1.3) connects **operations** to **finance (5.2)** and **break-even (5.4.4)**. Case questions often ask whether **automation** is justified.

sections.question: Garment maker in Country X (low wages) considers automated cutting machines costing $2m. Currently 200 cutters employed. Discuss factors in the decision.
sections.solution:
**Capital intensive pros:** Faster output, consistent quality, lower **unit labour cost** at high volume.

**Cons:** **$2m finance** (interest, payback — 10.3), **redundancy** and **retraining** (2.1), fixed costs if **fashion demand volatile**.

**Labour intensive pros:** **Flexibility** for small batches (4.1.4 job production), **low wages** reduce automation urgency.

**Decision factors:** **Forecast demand**, **labour cost trend**, **competitor automation**, **lead time** requirements.

sections.label: Mark a capital vs labour question
